steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$\frac{1}{3} \left(x+6\right)-\frac{1}{2} \left(x+6\right)=-8$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is $42$.

steps.title: Factor the repeated group
steps.content: Both terms contain $(x+6)$, so combine the coefficients first: $$\left(\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{2}\right)(x+6)=-8.$$

steps.title: Solve for $x$
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $-6$ to get $x+6=48$. Subtract $6$: $$x=42.$$ The key shortcut is combining the repeated $(x+6)$ group before distributing fractions. The answer is 42.
